channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Graduate Internship Opportunity – Embassy Role
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
An Embassy is offering a graduate internship opportunity for motivated candidates looking to gain hands-on experience in a diplomatic and international environment. About the Internship This internship provides exposure to the day-to-day operations of an embassy, offering valuable insight into diplomacy, international relations, and public service. Interns may: Support administrative and program activitiesAssist with research […]

The post Graduate Internship Opportunity – Embassy Role appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
An Embassy is offering a graduate internship opportunity for motivated candidates looking to gain hands-on experience in a diplomatic and international environment.

About the Internship

This internship provides exposure to the day-to-day operations of an embassy, offering valuable insight into diplomacy, international relations, and public service.

Interns may:

Support administrative and program activities
Assist with research and policy-related tasks
Contribute to communications and reporting
Engage in a collaborative, multicultural work environment

Who Should Apply

This opportunity is ideal for:

Recent graduates or final-year students
Individuals interested in diplomacy and international relations
Candidates with strong communication and organizational skills
Motivated individuals eager to gain professional experience

Key Details

Opportunity: Graduate Internship
Work Environment: Embassy / Diplomatic setting
Application Deadline: 6 May 2026

Why This Opportunity Matters

This internship offers a strong foundation for careers in diplomacy, international development, and global policy by providing real-world experience in an embassy setting.

How to Apply

Find full details and apply here.

For more Internship opportunities, Visit OFY Here.

The post Graduate Internship Opportunity – Embassy Role appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Racial Equity Research Grants – Call for Proposals
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Researchers are invited to apply for the Racial Equity Research Grants program, which supports academic research aimed at understanding and addressing racial inequality in education. About the Grant This grant funds rigorous academic research projects that contribute to advancing equity within education systems. The focus is on generating evidence that helps: Understand racial disparities in […]

The post Racial Equity Research Grants – Call for Proposals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers are invited to apply for the Racial Equity Research Grants program, which supports academic research aimed at understanding and addressing racial inequality in education.

About the Grant

This grant funds rigorous academic research projects that contribute to advancing equity within education systems.

The focus is on generating evidence that helps:

Understand racial disparities in education
Inform policies and interventions
Promote equitable outcomes for marginalized communities

What the Grant Supports

Eligible proposals must:

Be focused on education (broadly defined)
Include a strong research component
Demonstrate clear academic rigor and relevance

What Is Not Eligible

The program does not support:

Program evaluations without a research focus
Professional development activities
Curriculum development alone
Scholarships or capital projects
Research outside the field of education

Who Should Apply

This opportunity is ideal for:

Researchers with a doctoral degree (PhD or equivalent)
Academics and scholars in education or related fields
Individuals working on racial equity and inclusion in education

Key Details

Opportunity: Racial Equity Research Grants
Focus Area: Racial inequality in education
Eligibility: Doctorate holders
Application: Open (check link for deadlines and details)

Why This Opportunity Matters

This grant supports research that can shape policies and practices to reduce racial inequality in education, making it a powerful opportunity for scholars committed to social justice and systemic change.

How to Apply

Learn more and apply here.

For more Grant Opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post Racial Equity Research Grants – Call for Proposals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Fund Your Career Growth: SIPRI Internships 2026 in Stockholm (Global Opportunity for Future Peace Leaders)
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
If you are passionate about global peace, international relations, and development, the 2026 internship opportunities at the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute offer a unique chance to gain hands-on experience in one of the world’s most respected research institutions. Based in Solna, Stockholm, these internships are designed for motivated students and recent graduates eager to […]

The post Fully Fund Your Career Growth: SIPRI Internships 2026 in Stockholm (Global Opportunity for Future Peace Leaders) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are passionate about global peace, international relations, and development, the 2026 internship opportunities at the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute offer a unique chance to gain hands-on experience in one of the world’s most respected research institutions. Based in Solna, Stockholm, these internships are designed for motivated students and recent graduates eager to contribute to peacebuilding, policy, and global development efforts.

SIPRI is globally recognized for its research on conflict, security, and peace. Established in 1966, the institute provides critical data, analysis, and policy recommendations to governments, researchers, and international stakeholders.

Overview of SIPRI Internships 2026

SIPRI is offering five internship positions running from mid-July/August to mid-December 2026, across three dynamic teams:

1. Communication Team (2 Positions)

Interns in this team will:

  • Support the promotion of SIPRI’s research, publications, and events
  • Handle media inquiries and assist with communications strategies
  • Contribute to social media content and multimedia production
  • Support the 2026 Stockholm Forum on Peace and Development communication efforts
  • Assist in launching SIPRI’s major databases

You will collaborate closely with internal communication teams and external partners, gaining real-world experience in media and digital engagement.

2. Director’s Office (1 Position)

This role offers exposure to high-level decision-making and leadership processes.

Key responsibilities include:

  • Supporting internal planning and coordination
  • Assisting in high-level meetings and engagements
  • Drafting speeches, notes, and talking points
  • Organizing board meetings, events, and official visits

This is a fast-paced environment ideal for those interested in governance, policy, and executive support.

3. Stockholm Forum Team (2 Positions)

Interns will help organize the prestigious Stockholm Forum on Peace and Development, scheduled for 3–4 November 2026.

Responsibilities include:

  • Supporting event planning and logistics
  • Coordinating with global participants and stakeholders
  • Assisting in delivering a large-scale international conference
  • Providing on-site support during the forum

This forum gathers policymakers, researchers, activists, and experts worldwide to foster dialogue on peace and development.

Key Responsibilities Across Internships

All interns will engage in a variety of tasks such as:

  • Administrative and operational support
  • Information management and coordination
  • Communication with stakeholders
  • Meeting participation and note-taking
  • Event logistics and travel coordination
  • Content creation for digital platforms (especially for communication interns)

Eligibility Criteria and Requirements

To qualify, applicants must:

  • Be enrolled in a university internship program or affiliated with an organization
  • Have valid insurance and a designated supervisor/contact person
  • Be currently studying or recently graduated in fields such as:
    • International Relations
    • Political Science
    • Development Studies
    • Communication
    • Management

Additional Requirements:

  • Strong interest in peace, conflict, and development issues
  • Experience in coordination, administration, or event planning is an advantage
  • Excellent command of English (Swedish is a plus)
  • Strong organizational, communication, and teamwork skills
  • Proficiency in tools like Microsoft Office, Adobe Creative Suite, and social media platforms

What You Will Gain

Interning at SIPRI provides a rich learning experience, including:

  • Hands-on experience in international policy and operations
  • Exposure to global peacebuilding and development processes
  • Participation in organizing a major international conference
  • Skills in multimedia communication and digital content creation
  • Professional networking in a multicultural environment
  • Mentorship and structured learning support

Internship Conditions

  • Location: Stockholm, Sweden (with occasional remote work possibility)
  • Duration: Mid-July/August to mid-December 2026
  • Type: Full-time
  • Compensation: Unpaid (no coverage for travel, housing, or other expenses)
  • Supervision: Dedicated mentor assigned

Application Deadline

The final date to apply is:
Sunday, 10 May 2026 (23:59)

Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews in mid-to-late May 2026.

Why Choose SIPRI?

SIPRI is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity across gender, nationality, and cultural backgrounds. Interning here means joining a globally respected institution that actively shapes policies and discussions around peace and security.

How to Apply

Interested candidates can apply directly via the official SIPRI application portal: click here.

Learn more about this opportunity on the official website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth
Want to discover more Internship opportunities? Visit the OFY website: Click here.

The post Fully Fund Your Career Growth: SIPRI Internships 2026 in Stockholm (Global Opportunity for Future Peace Leaders) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Call for Trainers & Facilitators – Ashoka Arab World
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
The Ashoka Arab World is inviting applications for experienced trainers and facilitators to join its talent pool. This initiative supports Ashoka’s vision of building an “Everyone a Changemaker” world by equipping young people and social entrepreneurs with the skills to drive meaningful change. About the Opportunity Ashoka Arab World is building a network of professionals […]

The post Call for Trainers & Facilitators – Ashoka Arab World appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Ashoka Arab World is inviting applications for experienced trainers and facilitators to join its talent pool.

This initiative supports Ashoka’s vision of building an “Everyone a Changemaker” world by equipping young people and social entrepreneurs with the skills to drive meaningful change.

About the Opportunity

Ashoka Arab World is building a network of professionals who can deliver high-quality, engaging training programs across the region.

Selected trainers will support programs focused on:

Youth empowerment
Social entrepreneurship
Innovation and systems change

Areas of Expertise

They are particularly looking for trainers with experience in:

Entrepreneurship
Finance
Legal frameworks
Marketing
Digital skills
Leadership
Systems thinking

Who Should Apply

This opportunity is ideal for individuals who:

Have experience delivering participatory and engaging trainings
Are passionate about youth development and social impact
Have expertise in one or more of the listed areas
Are interested in working across the Arab region

Key Details

Opportunity: Trainers & Facilitators Pool
Organization: Ashoka Arab World
Focus: Youth empowerment and social entrepreneurship
Application: Open (rolling consideration)

Why This Opportunity Matters

By joining this pool, you contribute to shaping the next generation of changemakers across the Arab world, helping build skills, confidence, and innovative thinking among youth and social entrepreneurs.

How to Apply

Apply here.

For more Training Opportunities, Visit OFY Here.

The post Call for Trainers & Facilitators – Ashoka Arab World appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UK-German Study Visit 2026 in Wales: Fully Funded Teacher Exchange on Curriculum Innovation
item.dc:creator: Mercy N. Atsuki
item.description:
The UK-German Study Visit 2026 offers a powerful opportunity for teachers to engage in international collaboration, deepen their understanding of curriculum relevance, and gain practical insights into preparing students for life and work in a globalized world. Taking place in Wales from 25–27 June 2026, this fully funded professional development program brings together educators from […]

The post UK-German Study Visit 2026 in Wales: Fully Funded Teacher Exchange on Curriculum Innovation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The UK-German Study Visit 2026 offers a powerful opportunity for teachers to engage in international collaboration, deepen their understanding of curriculum relevance, and gain practical insights into preparing students for life and work in a globalized world. Taking place in Wales from 25–27 June 2026, this fully funded professional development program brings together educators from the UK and Germany in a dynamic, hands-on learning environment.

With a focus on real-world application, innovation, and cross-cultural exchange, this study visit is ideal for educators seeking to transform classroom experiences and implement meaningful curriculum strategies.

What Is the UK-German Study Visit About?

This funded initiative is designed to:

  • Bring together teachers from the UK and Germany
  • Explore how curriculum relevance can be effectively implemented in schools
  • Provide an international framework for understanding education systems and innovation

The program is organized by UK-German Connection in collaboration with LFEE, ensuring a high-quality and impactful experience for all participants.

Importantly:

  • No German language skills are required
  • Schools do not need to offer German as a subject
  • Open to teachers across all disciplines

Key Aims of the Study Visit

Participants will benefit from a comprehensive learning experience that includes:

1. Curriculum Development and Innovation

  • Explore how to design a whole-school curriculum that is meaningful and future-focused
  • Understand how curriculum can prepare students for real-life challenges and global careers

2. Insight into Welsh Education Practices

  • Gain exposure to policy and practical approaches in Wales
  • Learn how curriculum innovation is implemented successfully in schools

3. Professional Reflection and Exchange

  • Share experiences with fellow educators from the UK and Germany
  • Reflect on your own teaching practices and identify areas for growth

4. Practical Classroom Exposure

  • Visit Welsh schools and observe real classroom environments
  • Engage with host teachers and discuss best practices

5. Action Planning and Project Development

  • Develop practical project ideas that can be implemented in your school
  • Create actionable plans aligned with curriculum relevance

6. Digital Learning Tools

  • Discover digital tools that enhance curriculum delivery
  • Explore how technology can support international partnerships

7. Future Collaboration Opportunities

  • Learn about UK-German partnerships and funding opportunities
  • Receive guidance on building long-term international collaborations

Program Structure and Activities

The study visit offers a rich and interactive schedule, including:

  • Expert-led sessions from education specialists
  • Hands-on workshops
  • School visits across Wales
  • Professional dialogue and networking opportunities
  • Collaborative action-planning sessions

Participants are expected to attend the entire duration and actively engage in all activities.

Eligibility Criteria

This opportunity is open to:

  • Teachers of all subjects and positions
  • Educators in primary, secondary, and SEN schools
  • Schools that do not currently have a UK-German partnership

Additional requirements:

  • Strong support from your Headteacher or Principal
  • Commitment to implementing outcomes from the study visit
  • Interest in international collaboration and curriculum innovation

Location and Dates

  • Location: Wales, United Kingdom
  • Dates: Thursday, 25 June – Saturday, 27 June 2026
  • Optional travel days: Wednesday, 24 June and Sunday, 28 June 2026

Funding, Travel, and Costs

This is a fully funded opportunity, with the following coverage:

Covered:

  • Accommodation
  • Event-related costs
  • Travel reimbursement (after the event, per guidelines)

Not Covered:

  • Incidental expenses (e.g., snacks during travel, personal spending)

Additional support:

  • Up to 50% contribution toward supply cover costs (upon prior request)

Application Process

To apply:

  1. Complete the official application form HERE
  2. Submit your application before the deadline
  3. Await notification of results

Deadline

Tuesday, 28 April 2026 at 23:59 BST

Selection Notification

Successful applicants will be informed during the week beginning 11 May 2026.

Contact and Further Information

For inquiries, support, or additional details, contact:

  • Email: events@ukgermanconnection.org
  • For more information about this opportunity click HERE

Why You Should Apply

This study visit is more than a training—it’s an immersive experience that equips teachers with:

  • Practical tools for curriculum innovation
  • Exposure to international best practices
  • Opportunities for meaningful collaboration
  • A chance to influence the future of education in your school

If you are passionate about transforming education and preparing students for a global future, this opportunity is well worth pursuing.

To discover more study visit opportunities on OFY, click HERE

The post UK-German Study Visit 2026 in Wales: Fully Funded Teacher Exchange on Curriculum Innovation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: PhD Opportunity in Agricultural and Food Economics (Sweden 2026): Fully Funded Research on Sustainable Agriculture and Resilience
item.dc:creator: Mercy N. Atsuki
item.description:
Are you passionate about transforming agriculture through data, economics, and sustainability? A fully funded PhD position in Applied Economics (Agricultural and Food Economics) is now open at a globally respected institution in Sweden. This doctoral opportunity offers a unique chance to contribute to cutting-edge research focused on sustainable agricultural practices, resilience, and economic modelling. This […]

The post PhD Opportunity in Agricultural and Food Economics (Sweden 2026): Fully Funded Research on Sustainable Agriculture and Resilience appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about transforming agriculture through data, economics, and sustainability? A fully funded PhD position in Applied Economics (Agricultural and Food Economics) is now open at a globally respected institution in Sweden. This doctoral opportunity offers a unique chance to contribute to cutting-edge research focused on sustainable agricultural practices, resilience, and economic modelling.

This position is ideal for candidates eager to combine economic theory, applied econometrics, and real-world agricultural data to address pressing global challenges in food systems and environmental sustainability.

About the PhD Project

The doctoral research will focus on agricultural and food economics, specifically examining:

  • The adoption of sustainable agricultural production practices
  • Strategies to enhance resilience and preparedness in agriculture
  • The economic consequences of adopting such practices

Research Approach

The project is grounded in:

  • Microeconomic theory
  • Applied econometrics
  • Economic modelling

Data & Methodology

You will work with diverse datasets, including:

  • Farm-level panel data
  • Survey data
  • Registrar data

Additionally, the project may involve:

  • Designing and conducting independent surveys
  • Preparing and analysing existing datasets

This blend of theoretical and applied research ensures that your work contributes both academically and practically to agricultural transformation.

About the Position

As a PhD student, you will:

  • Conduct independent, supervised research leading to a doctoral thesis
  • Complete PhD coursework (90 credits)
  • Contribute to the academic environment through collaboration

Additional Responsibilities

  • Teaching and administrative duties (up to 20% of workload, if applicable)
  • Participation in departmental development

If you take on these additional responsibilities, your PhD duration may be extended accordingly.

Research Environment

You will join a vibrant research group specializing in Agricultural and Food Economics, which includes:

  • Around 20 researchers and staff
  • PhD students, postdoctoral researchers, and senior academics
  • Experts in:
    • Production economics
    • Consumer economics
    • Agricultural policy
    • Trade

Collaborative Opportunities

The department actively collaborates across disciplines, including:

  • Crop production
  • Animal health
  • Veterinary epidemiology
  • Agricultural technology

This creates a dynamic and interdisciplinary research environment.

About the Institution

The hosting university is internationally recognized for excellence in:

  • Life sciences
  • Environmental research
  • Sustainable development

With thousands of students and staff, the institution promotes:

  • Inclusivity and diversity
  • Gender equality
  • Innovation and scientific advancement

Programme Structure

  • Duration: 4 years (full-time)
  • Employment Type: Temporary doctoral position
  • Location: Uppsala, Sweden
  • Start Date: Upon agreement
  • Salary: Structured progression based on collective agreements

Eligibility Requirements

General Requirements

Applicants must meet one of the following:

  • Hold a Master’s degree (second-cycle qualification)
  • Have completed 240 credits, including at least 60 credits at second-cycle level
  • Possess equivalent qualifications from Sweden or abroad

Specific Requirements

  • Minimum 60 credits in Economics or related fields
  • Must include:
    • A degree project (thesis)
    • At least 15 credits at advanced level
  • Background in:
    • Agriculture
    • Natural resource economics
    • Forestry or similar fields

Language Requirement

  • Proof of English proficiency equivalent to Engelska B/6

Ideal Candidate Profile

The successful applicant should demonstrate:

  • Strong academic background in:
    • Agricultural economics
    • Applied economics
    • Economics or related disciplines
  • Knowledge of:
    • Research methods
    • Econometrics/statistical analysis
  • Excellent communication skills in English

Additional Merits

  • Experience in:
    • Scientific publishing
    • Agricultural systems (Swedish or European context)
    • Survey and registrar data analysis

Personal Attributes

  • Highly motivated and proactive
  • Independent thinker
  • Strong teamwork and collaboration skills

Application Process

Required Documents (PDF Format)

  1. Cover letter (motivation, research interests, skills)
  2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  3. Academic transcripts and degree certificates
  4. Master’s thesis (minimum 15 credits)
  5. Proof of English proficiency
  6. Contact details of two referees

Additional Requirement

  • Applicants with foreign citizenship must present a certified passport copy during the interview

Selection Criteria

Candidates will be evaluated based on:

  • Academic qualifications
  • Research potential
  • Ability to complete the PhD within the stipulated timeframe

Important Dates

  • Application Deadline: 30 April 2026
  • Publication Date: 27 March 2026

Why Apply?

This PhD offers:

  • Full-time funded research opportunity
  • Access to high-quality datasets and research tools
  • A collaborative, interdisciplinary environment
  • Career development in academia, policy, or international development
  • Contribution to global sustainability and food security

For more information about this opportunity, click HERE

How to Apply

Applicants must submit their applications through the official university application portal HERE.

discover more PHD opportunities on OFY HERE

The post PhD Opportunity in Agricultural and Food Economics (Sweden 2026): Fully Funded Research on Sustainable Agriculture and Resilience appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Global Youth Mental Health Research Fellowship 2026: Funded Program for Emerging Leaders
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Global Youth Mental Health Research Fellowship 2026 is a fully funded 12-month opportunity designed to support emerging leaders in child and adolescent mental health from Pakistan, Indonesia, and Colombia. This prestigious fellowship provides structured training, hands-on research experience, mentorship, and global collaboration opportunities for early-career professionals and individuals with lived experience who are committed […]

The post Global Youth Mental Health Research Fellowship 2026: Funded Program for Emerging Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Global Youth Mental Health Research Fellowship 2026 is a fully funded 12-month opportunity designed to support emerging leaders in child and adolescent mental health from Pakistan, Indonesia, and Colombia. This prestigious fellowship provides structured training, hands-on research experience, mentorship, and global collaboration opportunities for early-career professionals and individuals with lived experience who are committed to improving youth mental health outcomes.

About the Program

The Global Youth Mental Health Research Fellowship is implemented by the Global Institute of Human Development (GIHD) at Shifa Tameer-e-Millat University in collaboration with leading global institutions, including The George Washington University, Universitas Gadjah Mada, Universidad de los Andes, and The Global Health Network. The program aims to build the next generation of leaders who can address mental health challenges among young people through research, advocacy, and evidence-based practice.

This 12-month, full-time fellowship runs from 1 June 2026 to 30 May 2027. Fellows will be actively engaged in research projects under expert supervision, contributing to meaningful studies that inform policy and practice in global mental health. The program combines academic rigor with practical application, ensuring that participants gain both theoretical knowledge and real-world experience.

Participants will also benefit from a structured curriculum covering key areas such as global mental health foundations, research design, ethics and safeguarding, randomized controlled trials, data analysis, and dissemination of findings. Through this comprehensive approach, the fellowship equips participants with the tools needed to drive impactful change in their communities.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Youth mental health is a growing global priority, particularly in low- and middle-income countries where access to services and research capacity may be limited. This fellowship directly addresses these gaps by investing in local talent and strengthening research capabilities in key regions.

By focusing on early-career professionals and individuals with lived experience, the program ensures that diverse perspectives are included in shaping mental health solutions. This inclusive approach not only enhances the relevance of research but also promotes more effective and culturally responsive interventions.

The fellowship also fosters international collaboration, connecting participants with global experts and networks. This exposure helps fellows understand broader trends in mental health while contributing locally grounded insights to the global discourse.

What Participants Will Gain

Participants in the fellowship will gain a combination of academic, professional, and personal development benefits. The program offers financial support through a fellowship stipend, along with coverage for research-related expenses and travel, allowing fellows to focus fully on their work.

Fellows will develop advanced research skills through hands-on experience, working as part of multidisciplinary teams on ongoing projects. This practical exposure is complemented by mentorship from leading experts in global mental health, providing guidance on both technical and career development.

In addition, participants will receive training in grant writing and scientific publication, enhancing their ability to secure funding and disseminate their work effectively. The fellowship also provides access to a global network through The Global Health Network, enabling collaboration with professionals across more than 180 countries.

Upon successful completion, fellows will receive a certificate from the Global Mental Health Equity Lab at The George Washington University, adding significant value to their professional credentials and future career opportunities.

Who Can Apply

The fellowship is open to a diverse group of applicants who are committed to improving youth mental health. Eligible candidates include early-career psychiatrists, psychologists, academics in mental health, allied health professionals, and individuals working with young people in health or education settings.

Importantly, the program also welcomes young people with lived experience of mental health challenges, recognizing the value of lived perspectives in shaping research and advocacy efforts.

Applicants must have completed at least 16 years of education and/or have no more than five years of work experience in the mental health field. They must also be available to commit full-time for the entire duration of the fellowship.

Eligible Areas

Applicants must be based in Pakistan, Indonesia, or Colombia at the time of application.

Application Process and Deadline

Interested candidates must complete the online application form and submit all required materials before the deadline. The selection process includes shortlisting, interviews, and final evaluation.

The application deadline is 11 May 2026 at 5 pm local time. Shortlisted candidates will be notified on 18 May 2026, with interviews scheduled shortly after. Final decisions will be communicated by 29 May 2026, and the fellowship will commence on 1 June 2026.

Learn more and apply on the Global Health Network website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Global Youth Mental Health Research Fellowship 2026: Funded Program for Emerging Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Code for Africa AI for Good Fellowship 2026: Paid Ethical AI Opportunity for African Technologists
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Code for Africa AI for Good Fellowship 2026 offers a unique opportunity for technologists across Africa to apply their skills to real-world challenges while earning a stipend and building impactful solutions. Designed for mid-career professionals with experience in artificial intelligence and data science, this four-month fellowship supports the development of ethical, scalable AI tools […]

The post Code for Africa AI for Good Fellowship 2026: Paid Ethical AI Opportunity for African Technologists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Code for Africa AI for Good Fellowship 2026 offers a unique opportunity for technologists across Africa to apply their skills to real-world challenges while earning a stipend and building impactful solutions. Designed for mid-career professionals with experience in artificial intelligence and data science, this four-month fellowship supports the development of ethical, scalable AI tools that address pressing societal issues across the Sahel and neighbouring regions.

About the Program

The AI for Good Fellowship, implemented by Code for Africa (CfA) and supported by the Digitalise Youth consortium, is a fully funded program aimed at bridging the gap between technology and human rights work. The fellowship places selected participants in collaboration with Human Rights Defender (HRD) organisations, enabling them to co-create AI-driven solutions that strengthen civic engagement, counter misinformation, and support vulnerable communities.

Over the course of four months, fellows will design and prototype tools such as multilingual AI assistants, systems for tracking harmful narratives, and data-driven solutions that inform advocacy and policy decisions. Participants will also receive mentorship from CfA’s TechLab, DataLab, and AI Sandbox, as well as guidance from global experts in AI, policy, and civil society.

The program is highly practical, emphasizing real-world application and collaboration. Fellows will work directly with organisations operating in complex environments, ensuring that the solutions developed are context-sensitive, ethical, and scalable.

Why This Opportunity Matters

As artificial intelligence continues to shape societies globally, there is an increasing need for ethical and inclusive approaches to its development and deployment. This fellowship addresses that need by empowering African technologists to lead innovation in a way that prioritizes human rights, transparency, and social impact.

The Sahel region faces significant challenges, including disinformation, climate vulnerability, and restricted civic space. By equipping technologists with the tools and partnerships needed to address these issues, the fellowship contributes to building resilient digital ecosystems and strengthening democratic participation.

Importantly, the program also amplifies local expertise. By prioritizing applicants with strong ties to African communities, it ensures that solutions are grounded in lived realities rather than external assumptions.

What Participants Will Gain

Participants in the fellowship will benefit from both professional development and meaningful impact. The program offers hands-on experience in building AI solutions that respond to real challenges, allowing fellows to strengthen their technical and problem-solving skills.

Fellows will also gain access to mentorship from leading experts and institutions, helping them refine their approaches to ethical AI and responsible innovation. The collaborative environment fosters connections with policymakers, civil society organisations, and fellow technologists, expanding professional networks across the continent.

In addition, participants receive a monthly stipend of $500, providing financial support while they focus on their projects. By the end of the program, fellows will have developed tangible outputs, including prototypes, research briefs, and scalable implementation plans—valuable assets for career advancement.

Who Can Apply

The fellowship is open to mid-career technologists, including AI engineers, data scientists, and social innovators, who have demonstrated experience in using technology to address societal challenges.

Applicants should have strong technical skills in areas such as machine learning, natural language processing, or data analysis, as well as experience working with real-world datasets. A commitment to ethical AI practices, human rights, and inclusive design is essential.

Candidates must also demonstrate the ability to collaborate with diverse stakeholders, including civil society organisations and policymakers. Fluency in English, French, or Arabic is required, and familiarity with open-source tools or multilingual environments is considered an advantage.

Eligible Areas

Applicants must be based in one of the following countries: Benin, Burkina Faso, Cameroon, Chad, Ethiopia, Guinea, Mali, Mauritania, Niger, Senegal, Somalia, South Sudan, Sudan, or Togo.

Application Process and Deadline

Interested candidates must complete the online application form and submit the required information demonstrating their experience, technical expertise, and motivation to participate in the fellowship.

The application deadline is 11 May 2026, and early submission is encouraged. Selected fellows will join a cohort of innovators working at the intersection of AI and social impact, with opportunities to showcase their work to regional and global partners.

Learn more and apply on the official call for application.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Code for Africa AI for Good Fellowship 2026: Paid Ethical AI Opportunity for African Technologists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Join Student Energy Guided Projects Cohort 3 (Solar Stream): Build Real Solar Solutions and Transform Your Community
item.dc:creator: Hildah W. Nakafu
item.description:
The Student Energy Guided Projects Cohort 3 (Solar Stream) is now open for applications, offering an incredible opportunity for youth-led teams to gain hands-on experience in the clean energy sector while making a meaningful impact in their communities. This program is designed to empower young innovators with the tools, mentorship, and funding needed to design […]

The post Join Student Energy Guided Projects Cohort 3 (Solar Stream): Build Real Solar Solutions and Transform Your Community appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Student Energy Guided Projects Cohort 3 (Solar Stream) is now open for applications, offering an incredible opportunity for youth-led teams to gain hands-on experience in the clean energy sector while making a meaningful impact in their communities.

This program is designed to empower young innovators with the tools, mentorship, and funding needed to design and implement real-world solar photovoltaic (PV) projects. If your team is passionate about sustainability, climate action, and renewable energy, this is your chance to turn ideas into impactful solutions.

About the Guided Projects Program

The Student Energy Guided Projects initiative equips young people with critical early-career experience in the energy industry. Through this program, participants go beyond theory and actively engage in solving real community energy challenges.

Under the Solar Stream, selected teams will:

  • Identify pressing energy challenges within their communities
  • Design practical solar photovoltaic (PV) systems
  • Plan and execute installation projects
  • Deliver sustainable, community-driven energy solutions

This immersive experience ensures participants develop both technical and professional skills that are essential for thriving in the global clean energy transition.

Why You Should Apply

1. Hands-On Project Experience

Participants will gain real-world exposure by working through the entire project lifecycle, including:

  • Feasibility studies
  • System design and modeling
  • Budgeting and financial planning
  • Installation and commissioning

This practical learning approach bridges the gap between academic knowledge and industry application.

2. Mentorship, Training & Funding

Selected teams will benefit from:

  • Structured training modules
  • Guidance from industry experts and mentors
  • Access to funding of up to $15,000 to support project implementation

This support ensures that teams are well-equipped to execute impactful and scalable energy solutions.

3. Create Lasting Community Impact

Participants will:

  • Deliver real solar energy installations
  • Reduce energy costs and carbon emissions
  • Contribute to sustainable development in their communities

The projects are not just educational—they create measurable, long-term benefits.

Team-Based Application Requirement

Applications must be submitted as a team. Individual applications are not accepted.

Each team must:

  • Consist of 3–5 members
  • Bring together diverse and complementary skills such as:
    • Technical expertise
    • Project management
    • Financial planning
    • Communication and stakeholder engagement

Eligibility Criteria

Individual Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be 18–30 years old (as of March 2026)
  • Be fluent in English (program delivery language)
  • Commit 5–8 hours per week from May 2026 to September 2027
  • Have reliable internet access and a computer
  • Show a strong interest in:
    • Clean energy
    • Sustainability
    • Community development
  • Demonstrate background or interest in:
    • Climate
    • Energy
    • International cooperation

Team Requirements

Each team must:

  • Include 3–5 members
  • Have an identified or potential project site, such as:
    • Schools
    • Community centers
    • Farms
    • Non-profit facilities
  • Demonstrate a mix of relevant skills across the team

Program Timeline

The Guided Projects Cohort 3 runs for 18 months, from:

  • May 2026 to September 2027

Participants will progressively move through stages of project development, from planning to execution and final delivery.

Real Impact: What Past Cohorts Have Achieved

BeetBoxFarm Solar Project (Cohort 1)

  • Installed a 5 kW solar system on a farm in Ottawa
  • Generated projected returns of $35,000
  • Enabled reinvestment into sustainable farming practices

Solar EV Team Project (Cohort 2)

  • Developed a 2.18 kW solar installation
  • Generated over 2,500 kWh annually
  • Supported accessibility, inclusion, and cultural learning initiatives

Alumni Experiences

Participants consistently highlight the program’s value in building:

  • Leadership and teamwork skills
  • Technical expertise in solar energy systems
  • Budgeting and stakeholder management experience
  • Professional networks in the energy sector

Many alumni have gone on to expand their projects, secure additional funding, and scale their impact within their communities.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply is:

April 26th, 2026 at 11:59 PM Pacific Time

Late applications will not be considered, so ensure your team prepares and submits all required materials on time.

How to Apply

To apply, your team should:

  1. Form a group of 3–5 members
  2. Identify a potential project site
  3. Prepare a clear concept for your solar project
  4. Submit your application through the official Student Energy platform

APPLY HERE

About Student Energy

Student Energy is a global youth-led organization working with over 50,000 young people across 120+ countries. The organization focuses on empowering the next generation of leaders to accelerate the transition to a sustainable and equitable energy future.

Final Thoughts

The Guided Projects Cohort 3 is more than just a program—it is a launchpad for young leaders passionate about clean energy and sustainability. By combining technical training, mentorship, funding, and real-world implementation, it provides a rare opportunity to build impactful solutions while gaining valuable career experience.

If your team is ready to lead change and power the future, this is your moment.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here

The post Join Student Energy Guided Projects Cohort 3 (Solar Stream): Build Real Solar Solutions and Transform Your Community appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: The Carter Center Internships & Graduate Assistantships 2026: Paid Opportunities in Peace, Health, and Global Development (Atlanta, USA)
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Carter Center Internships and Graduate Assistantships 2026 offer exceptional opportunities for students and recent graduates to gain hands-on experience in global peacebuilding, public health, and international development. With over four decades of impact, The Carter Center has empowered nearly 4,000 interns and graduate assistants from around the world, equipping them with practical skills, professional […]

The post The Carter Center Internships & Graduate Assistantships 2026: Paid Opportunities in Peace, Health, and Global Development (Atlanta, USA) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Carter Center Internships and Graduate Assistantships 2026 offer exceptional opportunities for students and recent graduates to gain hands-on experience in global peacebuilding, public health, and international development. With over four decades of impact, The Carter Center has empowered nearly 4,000 interns and graduate assistants from around the world, equipping them with practical skills, professional exposure, and a strong foundation for impactful careers.

These programs are designed to cultivate the next generation of global leaders by providing immersive, real-world experiences in areas such as conflict resolution, human rights, international public health, and organizational operations. Participants not only contribute meaningfully to the Center’s mission but also gain valuable insights into career pathways in international development and policy.

About The Carter Center Educational Programs

The Carter Center’s Educational Programs are structured to provide:

  • Professional development opportunities
  • Exposure to global peace and health initiatives
  • Hands-on project experience
  • Mentorship from experts in international development

These programs serve as a launchpad for careers, enabling participants to transition into roles in government, international organizations, academia, and non-profits.

According to program leadership, the initiative is focused on preparing participants for future professional experiences while contributing to meaningful global work.

Program Tracks Available

The Carter Center offers two main opportunities under its Educational Programs:

1. Internship Program

The internship program provides students and recent graduates with the opportunity to:

  • Enhance academic and professional skills
  • Explore career options in relevant fields
  • Gain practical experience in global development

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet at least one of the following:

  • Undergraduate students (junior or senior level)
  • Graduate students (currently enrolled or recently enrolled)
  • Recent graduates (within 24 months of graduation)

Additional criteria include:

  • Strong academic performance
  • Demonstrated interest in peace, health, or related fields
  • Authorization to work in the United States

International students studying in U.S.-based institutions may apply, subject to institutional approval.

2. Graduate Assistantship Program

The Graduate Assistantship Program is designed for:

  • Master’s and doctoral students

Key Features:

  • Duration: 9–12 months
  • Work commitment: 20 hours per week
  • Compensation: Hourly paid

This program offers a deeper level of engagement and responsibility compared to internships, allowing participants to contribute more significantly to projects and initiatives.

Program Areas and Fields of Work

Participants can engage in a wide range of thematic areas, categorized into three main sectors:

1. Peace Programs

  • Conflict Resolution
  • Democracy and Governance
  • Human Rights
  • Rule of Law
  • Regional Focus Programs (e.g., Latin America, China)

2. Health Programs

  • International Public Health
  • Mental Health
  • Specialized Health Projects

3. Operations and Support Functions

  • Communications
  • Human Resources
  • Information Technology
  • Strategy and Innovation
  • Development and Fundraising
  • Volunteer Services

These diverse program areas allow participants to align their academic background and career interests with real-world initiatives.

Compensation and Benefits

The Carter Center provides financial support to interns and graduate assistants:

Internship Stipends

  • Spring (15 weeks): $5,100
  • Summer (10 weeks): $3,400
  • Fall (15 weeks): $5,100

Graduate Assistantships

  • Hourly compensation for 20 hours per week

Additional benefits include:

  • Professional mentorship
  • Networking opportunities with global experts
  • Real-world experience in international development
  • Exposure to high-level policy and operational work

Program Location

All internship and graduate assistantship opportunities are based in:

  • Atlanta, Georgia, United States

Participants are expected to work on-site unless otherwise specified.

Application Deadlines and Important Dates

Internship Deadlines

  • Summer 2026
    • Deadline: March 2, 2026 (11:59 PM ET)
    • Start Date: May 19, 2026
  • Fall 2026
    • Deadline: June 15, 2026 (11:59 PM ET)
    • Start Date: August 25, 2026

Graduate Assistantship Deadlines

  • Application Opens: February 13, 2026
  • Application Deadline: April 23, 2026 (11:59 PM ET)
  • Recommendation Letters Deadline: April 30, 2026 (11:59 PM ET)

Real Impact and Career Outcomes

The Carter Center programs have a strong track record of shaping future leaders.

Participants often:

  • Transition into full-time roles within the Center
  • Pursue advanced degrees at top institutions
  • Secure positions in government and international organizations

For example, former interns have contributed to election observation missions and international development projects, later advancing to graduate studies and global careers.

How to Apply

Applicants can apply directly through the Carter Center’s Official Application Portal.

Application Steps:

  1. Select your preferred program (Internship or Graduate Assistantship)
  2. Prepare required documents (resume, academic records, etc.)
  3. Submit your application before the deadline
  4. Await communication regarding selection

Contact Information

For inquiries and eligibility clarification:

  • Email: EducationalPrograms@CarterCenter.org

Applicants are encouraged to reach out for guidance, especially regarding work authorization requirements.

Click HERE to access the program’s FAQs.

Learn more about the Carter Center Internships and Graduate Assistantships 2026 HERE.

Conclusion

The Carter Center Internships and Graduate Assistantships 2026 provide a powerful platform for students and young professionals to gain meaningful experience in global peace and health initiatives.

With competitive stipends, access to world-class expertise, and opportunities to contribute to impactful projects, these programs stand out as premier pathways into careers in international development and public service.

Applicants are encouraged to prepare early and submit strong applications before the respective deadlines.

Discover more global Internship opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post The Carter Center Internships & Graduate Assistantships 2026: Paid Opportunities in Peace, Health, and Global Development (Atlanta, USA) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
